summary_one_line: A LOG_REPHA Repha-leading syllable of <=2 glyphs so has_reph is set, base==start, and limit-base<=2 hold at the true branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Font/buffer bytes that classify the first glyph as an Indic Repha under REPH_MODE_LOG_REPHA (line 534 taken), giving has_reph=true and base=start, with a syllable so short that the backward base-search adds no further consonant, so limit-base<=2 and the true branch fires.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds drive the [W] LOG_REPHA arm at lines 534-541 (limit+=1; base=start; has_reph=true) and never enter the [L] consonant-found arm at 550-558 that would move base off start; at the blocker line 595 the winner shows T=6 F=0 while the loser shows T=0, so the winner alone takes the true side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds fall through to the backward base-search and hit the [L] is_consonant / set-base arm (lines 550-558, base=i), so base!=start and the line-595 conjunction is false; the loser's line-595 counts are T=0 F=0 / T=0 F=14, never the true side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive's plain havoc+token byte mutation stumbles onto the short Repha-leading glyph configuration; cmplog's input-to-state CMP-operand substitution adds no help here (the gate is a position/category-classification predicate on already-shaped glyphs, not an integer/string CMP whose operand could be substituted), and the I2S mutator's bias appears to steer cmplog away from the rare short-syllable layout, so the non-I2S havoc-only baseline wins.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the conjunction has_reph && base==start && limit-base<=2 whose true side is globally blocked.
The winner is naive and the loser is cmplog, with the delta being the I2S technique.
naive resolves all 10 trials while cmplog blocks 8 of 10.
The winner-only LOG_REPHA path sets has_reph and base=start, satisfying the first two conjuncts.
The winner LOG_REPHA arm assigns base = start so base==start holds.
The loser instead reaches the consonant-found arm that reassigns base to an interior index, breaking base==start.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side six times and the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 595  T=6 F=0  T=0 F=0  if (has_reph && base == start && limit - base <= 2) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner-resolving seeds were produced by naive using only havoc/copy byte mutators, with no I2S mutator in their op chain.
naive has no I2S mutator; its observed mutation ops are havoc/token only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace works by substituting CMP operands matched in the input, i.e. it targets comparison operands, not a glyph-position classification predicate.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not applicable: the winner is naive (no I2S), so claimed_mechanism is havoc-only, not I2SRandReplace; lineage dash-row verification is only mandated for an I2S-attributed winner. Winner seeds (e.g. 8a40b6da90271295) carry pure havoc op chains (ByteFlipMutator,BytesCopyMutator) with no I2S floor row, consistent with a havoc-only attribution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness exposing this gate where enabling cmplog's I2S substitution raises the rate of taking the true branch at or above naive's rate would refute the claim that I2S provides no advantage (and is mildly detrimental) at this position-classification predicate.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that I2S actively steers cmplog AWAY from the short-syllable layout (rather than merely being neutral) is inferred from the trial split, not from direct seed-byte evidence.
